critic_raw:
Four issues: preprocessing leakage from full-dataset fitting, temporal leakage from random split, test-set contamination through architecture refinement, and feature asymmetry between models.

Issue 3 is the most severe: Section 6 explicitly states the test set is used to 'fine-tune model architecture if discrepancies are observed.' This is an unambiguous statement of test-set misuse that invalidates the test estimate.
all_issues_raised: Preprocessing transformers fit on entire dataset before splitting — data leakage into test set
Stratified random split on 5-year longitudinal EHR data ignores temporal ordering — temporal leakage
Test set used for model architecture refinement — explicit violation: 'fine-tune model architecture if discrepancies are observed' invalidates test set
RNN receives richer sequence-level features unavailable to logistic regression baseline — feature asymmetry confounds architectural comparison

round_transcripts.critic:
Four issues: preprocessing leakage from full-dataset fitting, temporal leakage from random split, test-set contamination through architecture refinement, and feature asymmetry between models.

Issue 3 is the most severe: Section 6 explicitly states the test set is used to 'fine-tune model architecture if discrepancies are observed.' This is an unambiguous statement of test-set misuse that invalidates the test estimate.
round_transcripts.defender:
**On Issue 1 (Preprocessing leakage):** Confirmed by explicit design text. Transformers fit on full dataset.

**On Issue 2 (Temporal split):** Confirmed. Random stratified split on longitudinal EHR data violates temporal causal ordering.

**On Issue 3 (Test-set misuse):** This is confirmed by the design text and is the most severe flaw. The phrasing 'fine-tune model architecture if discrepancies are observed' using test-set results is an explicit statement of test-set contamination. The test set cannot be used as an unbiased evaluator if it was used to guide model decisions. This issue is unambiguously valid and severe.

**On Issue 4 (Feature asymmetry):** The RNN does receive substantially richer input (12-visit sequences with codes and medications) compared to logistic regression (aggregated summary statistics). Any observed improvement could be due to feature richness rather than sequence modeling. This is a valid confound for architectural interpretation.

round_transcripts.critic: The defense concedes all four issues. All four remain unresolved. Issue 3 — test-set misuse — is a fatal methodological flaw that invalidates the experiment's primary claim. Issues 1, 2, and 4 are additional compounding problems.
round_transcripts.defender: All four issues confirmed. Issue 3 is fatal: explicit test-set misuse for architecture refinement. Issues 1, 2, and 4 are additive methodological problems that would remain even if Issue 3 were corrected.

all_issues_adjudicated: Preprocessing transformers fit on entire dataset before splitting — data leakage
Stratified random split on 5-year longitudinal EHR data ignores temporal ordering
Test set used for model architecture refinement — explicit statement in Section 6 invalidates test set as unbiased evaluator
RNN receives richer sequence-level features unavailable to logistic regression — feature asymmetry confounds architectural comparison
